items.title: Wife and carer 'dumbfounded' by husband's aged care assessment

children.content: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he's been refused funding for more support.

gallery.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with light blonde hair in a pink shirt rests her hand on the head of a bald man in a chair with an oxygen mask on.

avatar.alt: Sacha Payne, dressed in a red jacket and white shirt, stands outside Victorian Parliament.

items.title: Girl, 2, dies in four-vehicle crash in West Melbourne

children.content: A two-year-old
girl has died after a four-vehicle crash on a major arterial route in inner-city Melbourne this afternoon.

image.altText: A crashed car.

items.title: Media banned from showing faces of alleged RSL vandals

children.content: A court has suppressed the images of two women accused of graffitiing two RSLs in Melbourne for 12 months after their case is finalised.

image.altText: Pink spraypaint on a white brick wall beside a glass front of a building with a curved sign that says "RSL on Bell".

items.title: Virginia Trioli to leave the ABC after 27 years

children.content: After 27 years as a journalist, broadcaster and program maker at the ABC, Virginia Trioli announces she is leaving to pursue new creative opportunities.

gallery.altText: Virginia Trioli, wearing a fitted blazer, poses for a photo in a studio next to a spotlight and microphone

thumbnail.altText: Virginia Trioli, wearing a fitted blazer, poses for a photo in a studio next to a spotlight and microphone

items.title: 'ISIS bride' accused of owning a slave in Syria refused bail

children.content: A woman accused of slavery offences while supporting the Islamic State group in Syria has had her bail application refused.

image.altText: A court sketch of Zeinab Ahmad

items.title: Gold Coast Suns hit with massive fine for umpire contact

children.content: The AFL hands down a fine to the Suns after the club's fifth incident this season of a player making careless contact with an umpire.

image.altText: Daniel Rioli handballs for the Gold Coast Suns during a 2026 AFL match.

items.title: AFL stars' mother recounts visit from Dezi Freeman detectives

children.content: Kay Reid, a regional Victorian businesswoman and the mother of footballers Ben and Sam Reid, confirms police searched her home as part of Taskforce Summit's investigation into the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman.

gallery.altText: A police officer stands near two others who sit at a table with a bald man in a green shirt who look directly at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer stands near two others who sit at a table with a bald man in a green shirt who look directly at the camera.

items.title: Life terms introduced for 'evil' Victorian gangs who recruit children

children.content: The Victorian government is raising the maximum penalty for recruiting children to commit crimes as it seeks to shut down organised crime syndicates driving the state's crime wave.

image.altText: a screenshot of cctv showing three people outside a home in black and white

items.title: Church youth leader who assaulted minor 'knew it was wrong', court told

children.content: Joshua Emonson, 24, earlier pleaded guilty to sexually assaulting a 13-year-old girl near Sunbury train station in February.

image.altText: Joshua Emonson poses for a photo with four other people

items.title: Marauding cockatoos thwarted by bin lid invented by men's shed

children.content: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

gallery.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".
Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.
A white haired man in a red top and jeans holds a dark plastic square over the top of an open wheelie bin.

thumbnail.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".
Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.

items.title: Dealing with NDIS leading to anxiety and suicidal ideation among participants

children.content: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

image.altText: Andrew Bretherton dressed in pants and dark jumper and jacket sits in a wheelchair.

items.title: Advocates call for a rethink on the factors driving male suicide

children.content: There are urgent calls for more customised support services for men and boys experiencing situational distress.

image.altText: close uup of Paul Withall

items.title: Melbourne gym targeted by arsonists two days in a row

children.content: Police are investigating the second suspicious fire at a South Melbourne gym in as many days.

image.altText: A fire truck outside a burning building.

items.title: Farmer fertiliser hope as Iran ceasefire promises to reopen supply

children.content: Farmers are hoping the US-Iran deal will help free up fertiliser supply and lower costs during the winter cropping season.

image.altText: A hand holds brilliant white fertiliser while a truck tips it off in the background

items.title: Birth doula says lack of consent delayed Triple Zero call for dying mother

children.content: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

gallery.altText: Stacey Warnecke, who died after giving birth at home, smiles for a publicity photo in her kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: Stacey Warnecke, who died after giving birth at home, smiles for a publicity photo in her kitchen.

items.title: Jacinta Allan refuses to admit cost of CFMEU corruption to taxpayers

children.content: Embattled Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan has refused to answer repeated questions over how much CFMEU corruption has cost taxpayers in the state, a scandal that has put the government and her leadership under threat.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses holds a document while speaking to reporters in a conference room.

items.title: Two men arrested by Dezi Freeman investigators

children.content: Police executed seven warrants at properties across Victoria and New South Wales this morning.

image.altText: A screen grab of a man looking seriously at a the camera.

items.title: Trio guilty of murdering teen Ethan Hoac in 'appalling' machete attack

children.content: A third man has been found guilty of murdering 14-year-old Ethan Hoac in an unprovoked machete attack in Melbourne's north-west in 2023.

image.altText: Teen Ethan Hoac was killed when he was 14 in 2023. He is pictured here smiling outside and making the OK symbol.

items.title: Craft retailer Lincraft to close rest of its stores in Australia and NZ

children.content: Retailer Lincraft will close the remainder of its shopfronts after more than 80 years of providing fabric, materials and homewares to Aussie crafters.

image.altText: A large white-on-blue 'Lincraft' sign hangs over a storefront, with the tag line 'where ideas come to life'.

items.title: Beer microbiologist's orchid hobby helping document rare species

children.content: Robert Mitchell's interest in rare orchids in the area around Frankston results in him naming three and penning a research paper.

gallery.altText: A man crouches, snapping a picture of a plant with a camera.
A collage showing six different native orchid types.

items.title: PE teacher 'totally without remorse' sentenced for historical sex offences

children.content: The court hears William Gilfillan, more commonly known as Rob, will be 92 years old when he becomes eligible for parole, and he will likely die in jail.

image.altText: Black and white image of a man holding a sports trophy

items.title: Afghan refugee climbs Mount Everest in world first to inspire girls

children.content: River Ahmad fled Afghanistan in 2018. She has now conquered the world's highest mountain with the dream of helping educate girls in her home country.

image.altText: River smiles while sitting in the snow on Mt Everest

items.title: Regulator finds kids potentially at 'catastrophic' risk in 12th floor daycare

children.content: An inspection of a childcare centre in inner Melbourne found faults in the fencing meant children could potentially access a balcony.

image.altText: A pathway that looks like a road winds through fake grass around an aeroplane in an indoor playground.

items.title: VicRoads chief 'sorry' over week-long website outages

children.content: VicRoads has apologised after a week-long series of technological glitches meant motorists could not register new vehicles or book driving tests.

image.altText: Man in shirt with Giles Thompson caption below

items.title: Allied health workers walk off the job at Victorian public hospitals

children.content: The strike involves radiographers, sonographers, physiotherapists, podiatrists, speech pathologists and social workers at the state's public hospitals.

gallery.altText: A large crowd of people holding signs and banners including one saying "respect allied health" stand on a city road.

thumbnail.altText: A large crowd of people holding signs and banners including one saying "respect allied health" stand on a city road.

items.title: Hospital call from Melbourne's outer north leaders

children.content: A group of councils in Melbourne's north is calling on the state government to spend $45 million to buy land to begin planning for a hospital and train station.

items.title: Icy atmosphere as Victorian MPs raise funds for MND

children.content: State government and opposition MPs take the plunge to raise tens of thousands of dollars for motor neurone disease research just days after Neale Daniher's state funeral.

items.title: Cancer patient in remission after 'amazing' outcome of targeted trial

children.content: Ben Whitehead was given just months to live after being diagnosed with a rare cancer that doctors had not seen before. A second clinical trial has delivered hope.

items.title: Disgraced former AFL footballer jailed for pool fraud

children.content: An ex-footballer has been locked up after a jury found him guilty of defrauding multiple families with his regional pool business.

items.title: Former police officer steps aside from country football over sexual assault charges

children.content: A female footballer has "stepped aside" from her country Victoria club after sexual assault allegations against her were revealed.

items.title: Police investigate spectator brawl during country Victorian football match

children.content: Victoria Police is investigating an off-field fight at a local football match in Ararat over the weekend, sparking concerns about "dreadful" behaviour at grassroots sporting events.

items.title: Jubilant fans pack Federation Square to celebrate Socceroos win

children.content: In Melbourne, Federation Square became the epicentre of fan festivities, with capacity crowds packing into the CBD venue for a Socceroos viewing party.

articles.title: The Socceroos' greatest ever World Cup gamble just paid off

children.content: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.
